description: CTE result used in a LEFT JOIN, making right-side columns nullable

columns.name: total
columns.note: RIGHT side of LEFT JOIN: becomes nullable

columns.name: notes
columns.note: Already nullable in schema AND on right side of LEFT JOIN

name: cte_left_join
query_sql:
-- @name GetActiveUsersWithOptionalOrders
-- @returns :many
WITH active AS (SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE status = 'active') SELECT a.id, a.name, o.total, o.notes FROM active a LEFT JOIN orders o ON a.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', bio TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly marks o.total as nullable because it is on the right side of a LEFT JOIN
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks o.total as non-nullable because orders.total is NOT NULL
